# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { RequestHandler } from 'express';
const express = require('express');
var cors = require("cors"); // You can also use other packages like "cross-origin" if needed.
// Import the required modules/packages: bodyParser and nodeFetch (to fetch data for streaming). 
import { streamResponse } from 'streams';  
const app = express();
app.use(cors()); // Apply CORS to enable all cross origin requests with credentials headers, etc... like req-1 in your requirement is required only if you want a way of setting cors on server side not when calling it locally through client and then we can use streamResponse() as per need..
app.use(express.json({ limit: '50mb' })); // You may increase the JSON request body size here based your requirement 
const fetch = require('node-fetch');  
// Fetching User Provided Data from Post Request Body and Streams it back to client via Server Response using Vercel Infrastructure as Code (Now)... this is how you stream data in response of express server, just pass the necessary information into .send() method. 
app.post('/streamData', async function(req: any , res){    // Express RequestHandler type definition - we can extend it if needed based on our needs   });     }) as unknown) as typeof import("express").RequestHandler;      const { StreamResponse } = require('@vercel/node');
app.post('/streamData', (async function(req, res){    // Express Request Handler type definition - we can extend it if needed based on our needs   });     })  at the end of your code: */'+your_response; return Promise((resolve) => { resolve = null }; var streamResponseBody= Buffer.from('').toString("base64");
